data.title: Jim Beam to idle Kentucky distillery as bourbon demand slows
data.description:
Jim Beam will suspend production at its Clermont, Kentucky, distillery for a year as sluggish spirits sales, costly import tariffs, and changing consumer tastes weigh on the world’s largest bourbon maker.

While the company said it will use this time to invest in “site enhancements,” it acknowledged that demand has not met supply.

“We are always assessing production levels to best meet consumer demand and recently met with our team to discuss our volumes for 2026,” the company said in a statement.

Jim Beam is owned by Suntory Global Spirits, an entity of Japan’s Suntory Group.

The closure – albeit temporarily – underscores the challenges faced by the spirits industry. A recent Gallup poll found that the number of adults who consume alcohol has dropped to a 90-year low, while an existing ban on U.S. spirit sales in Canada drove exports of American-made bourbon into Canada down 85%.

Exports to the UK and Japan each declined by more than 23%, while exports to the EU were down 12%. Canada, Japan, the EU and the UK accounted for 70% of all U.S. spirits exported in 2024. And as American whiskey inventory has tripled since 2012, exports have been critical to the industry to compensate for declining domestic sales.

“Total distilled spirits has weakened in the last 6 months or so, not dramatically, but it has,” said Brown-Forman CEO Lawson Whiting on the company’s most recent  earnings call responding to a question about holiday sales. “Our expectations for this holiday season are—we're trying to be optimistic, but at the same time, we are looking and seeing some of the macro trends that are tough."

Brown-Foreman makes Jack Daniel’s, Woodford Reserve, and Old Forester bourbons.

While Diageo -- parent company of Johnnie Walker whiskey -- lowered FY26 sales forecasts to compensate for a weaker U.S. consumer environment “than originally planned for.”

“We are not satisfied with our current performance and are focused on what we can manage and control; acting with speed to drive efficiencies, prioritizing investment and adapting more quickly to an evolving consumer environment,” Diageo interim CEO Nik Jhangiani said.

For bourbon drinkers, the decision to trim new production at Jim Beam will not impact future supply with the Kentucky distilleries currently aging a record 16.1 million barrels of bourbon, the Kentucky Distillers’ Association says, up 27% from last year and the highest level since the repeal of Prohibition in 1933.

Shares of Brown-Foreman (BF-A) (BF-B), and Diageo (DEO) are both lower on Tuesday.

data.title: Hycroft among top S&P gainers after higher-grade Vortex silver report
data.description:
• Hycroft Mining Holding (HYMC) jumped 5.6% Tuesday morning, ranking among the S&P’s top gainers, following a 49% surge in the previous session
  • The company announced drill results from the 2025-2026 exploration drill program on Monday at the Hycroft Mine, located in Nevada, USA, a tier-1 mining jurisdiction. Reports the highest grades to date identified in Vortex.
  • Vortex expands approximately 70 meters to the northwest and approximately 90 meters down-dip to the west based on results from H25D-6070 and H25D-6072.
  • Hycroft (HYMC) shares have more than doubled over the past month, gaining 75% in the last five days alone.

data.title: Pfizer reports patient death in hemophilia trial for Hympavzi
data.description:
• Pfizer (PFE) on Tuesday announced that a patient who was taking part in a long-term extension trial for its hemophilia therapy Hympavzi (marstacimab) died after serious adverse events.
  • The patient died in December following serious adverse events of cerebellar infarction and subsequent cerebral hemorrhage, Pfizer (PFE) said, adding that further investigations are ongoing to understand the events that led to his death.
  • In 2024, the U.S. FDA approved Hympavzi as a once-weekly subcutaneous prophylactic treatment to prevent or reduce the frequency of bleeding episodes in people with hemophilia A or B.
  • The New York-based company was evaluating the drug’s long-term safety and efficacy in hemophilia patients who had completed its BASIS and BASIS KIDS Phase 3 trials for marstacimab. The patient in question had completed the BASIS trial before entering the long-term extension study in 2023, according to Pfizer (PFE).

data.title: Booking Holdings is seeing improved web traffic in Q4
data.description:
Booking Holdings (BKNG) is seeing momentum in the fourth quarter, according to a data dive by Wedbush Securities.

Analyst Scott Devitt and his team pointed to positive quarter-to-date trends through the end of November relative to expectations. The analyst team noted that web traffic to Booking on a consolidated level grew +8% year-over-year QTD, which was an acceleration of about 600 basis points from the third-quarter trend. "Booking app MAU growth accelerated ~100 bps from 3Q, primarily supported by a sequential improvement across Booking.com, Agoda, and Kayak. We believe web and app engagement trends are directionally encouraging versus expectations, which currently call for a ~220 bps deceleration in room night growth to +5.9% Y/Y (from +8.0% in 3Q)," highlighted Devitt.

Shares of Booking Holdings (BKNG) edged 0.1% higher in Tuesday morning trading to $5,410.61 vs. the 52-week range of $4,096.23 to $5,839.41.

data.title: U.S. offshore wind halt seen as short-lived, Goldman Sachs says
data.description:
A recent stop-work order affecting large U.S. offshore wind projects is likely to have a temporary effect on Dominion Energy (D) and Eversource Energy (ES), despite near-term uncertainty and sharp initial stock reactions, according to analysts at Goldman Sachs.

On December 22, the U.S. Department of the Interior issued an immediate suspension of work on leases for all large-scale offshore wind projects currently under construction. The order applies to several major developments, including Dominion Energy’s (D) Coastal Virginia Offshore Wind project and Revolution Wind, which is owned by Ørsted (DNNGY) (DOGEF) and Global Infrastructure Partners.

The Department of the Interior cited national security concerns related to radar interference from turbine blades and tower structures. While the announcement did not specify a duration, both Dominion Energy (D) and Ørsted (DNNGY) (DOGEF) indicated the suspension is expected to last about 90 days, with the Bureau of Ocean Energy Management retaining the option to extend the pause.

Shares of Dominion Energy (D) and Eversource Energy (ES) fell following the announcement, but Goldman Sachs said the underlying impact on both companies should be limited. Analysts led by Carly Davenport noted that the affected projects are close to completion and that prolonged suspension or cancellation would carry significant implications for electricity supply and customer affordability in Virginia and New England.

Dominion’s (D) Coastal Virginia Offshore Wind project is expected to deliver first power in early 2026 and is already incorporated into customer rates. The project is regulated, with revenue requirements approved through August 2026 and additional recovery filings underway. As a result, Goldman Sachs said the stop-work order should not affect Dominion’s (D) 2025 financial guidance.

Revolution Wind, which is approximately 90% complete, is expected to supply electricity to Rhode Island and Connecticut, where electricity bills have risen sharply in recent years. Although Eversource (ES) sold its 50% stake in the project in 2024, the company could face additional financial obligations if the project were ultimately canceled. Goldman Sachs noted that Eversource (ES) has already recorded liabilities related to potential adjustments and that any short-term suspension would likely have a limited incremental cost.

The analysts also pointed to recent precedent, noting that similar stop-work orders issued earlier this year for Empire Wind and Revolution Wind were lifted after roughly one month following legal or administrative review.

Beyond Dominion (D) and Eversource (ES), the suspension affects several other near-completion projects, including Vineyard Wind, Sunrise Wind and Empire Wind, raising broader questions about permitting risk for offshore wind developments in the U.S.

Goldman Sachs maintained a Neutral rating on Dominion Energy (D) and a Buy rating on Eversource Energy (ES), citing differences in valuation, regulatory exposure and balance-sheet positioning. The firm said longer-term risks include regulatory uncertainty, construction execution challenges and the evolving federal policy environment for offshore wind.

data.title: Consumer confidence slips after unusually large upward revision for November
data.description:
U.S. Consumer Confidence Index: -3.8 to 89.1 vs. 91.9 consensus and 92.9 in November (revised from 88.7), according to data released by The Conference Board on Tuesday.

The revision to November's numbers reflect responses collected after the end of the federal government shutdown (which spanned Oct. 1 to Nov. 12) were more positive than those collected during the impasse.

Present situation index: -9.5 to 116.8.

Expectations: 70.7, steady with prior month.

Developing... check back for updates.

data.title: MGM Resorts International announces new long-term branding agreement with MGM China
data.description:
• MGM Resorts International (MGM) has entered into a new long term branding agreement with MGM China (MCHVF) for a term through the end of the current concession in 2032, effective January 1, 2026. 
  • The firm said the term will be automatically extended until the earlier of the expiration of a new concession or December 31, 2045, provided a further concession is granted or awarded.
  • The new monthly license fee has increased from 1.75% to 3.5% of MGM China's adjusted consolidated net monthly revenues, the company said.
  • The fees will be subject to an annual cap determined by certain variables, as required by the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, the most significant being business volumes of MGM China. As part of this agreement, MGM Resorts will receive approximately 66.6% of the license fee, it added.
  • The agreement secures an important right for MGM China, as the MGM brand has served it well, which is reflected in the significant gains in market share and increased profitability since the end of the pandemic. Market share has nearly doubled from a pre-pandemic level of approximately 9% to a year-to-date September 30, 2025, share of approximately 16%. Further, the new agreement eliminates the need for the parties to negotiate a new agreement every three years.
  • MGM is -0.61% to $37.32.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Edwards Lifesciences wins FDA nod for SAPIEN M3 TMVR system
data.description:
Edwards Lifesciences (EW) announced on Tuesday that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration approved its SAPIEN M3 transcatheter mitral valve replacement ((TMVR)) system for patients with mitral regurgitation, a common valvular heart disease.

Accordingly, the SAPIEN M3 TMVR system will be available in the U.S. for symptomatic moderate-to-severe or severe MR in patients for whom surgery or transcatheter edge-to-edge repair ((TEER)) therapy is not indicated, the company said.

It is also available for patients with symptomatic mitral valve dysfunction linked to mitral annular calcification, if those patients are found to be ineligible for TEER therapy or surgery.

The California-based MedTech added that SAPIEN M3 is the first transcatheter therapy using a transseptal approach to be available in the U.S. for mitral regurgitation.

In April, Edwards Lifesciences (EW) received Europe’s CE Mark for the SAPIEN M3 system, making it the world’s first TAVR system utilizing a transfemoral approach approved to treat MR.

data.title: Wall Street searches for direction as traders weigh GDP data in holiday-shortened week
data.description:
Wall Street’s major market averages search for clear direction on Tuesday as investors parsed through delayed economic growth data on the holiday shortened trading week.

The tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.2%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was +0.1%, and the blue-chip Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJI) was near even.

From a sector vantage point, seven of the S&P 500’s 11 sectors are trading up in the green with Communication Services at the top. At the other end, Consumer Staples have been the weakest performer so far. 

“The stock market is finally starting to eke out some gains for December after a choppy few weeks, and just in time for the market's Santa Claus rally, which we expect to take place in its typical format via the last several trading days of the year,” Paul Stanley, chief investment officer of Granite Bay Wealth Management said. 

“The combination of low volume and an absence of bad news should keep the Santa Claus rally alive and well for the rest of 2025,” Stanley added. 

The Santa Claus rally refers to a historical trend that has seen the benchmark S&P 500 index post gains during the last five trading days of the year and the first two of the next year. 

On the economic calendar, U.S. Q3 GDP annualized growth of +4.3% blew past the consensus figure of +3.2%. However, delving past the topline beat, the report showed some weakness. 

"The main issue in the Q3 data is the strong performance of household consumption against weak real incomes. Ex-transfers, real income is flat over the last two quarters while consumption is expanding 3%. Either income is being underreported or we're seeing an unsustainable drawdown in savings," Renaissance Macro Research said on X. 

Furthermore, durable goods orders fell more than expected in the month of October. 

U.S. Treasury yields advanced across the curve. The shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) moved up 4 basis points to 3.55%, while the U.S.10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 3 basis points to 4.19%. 

As for stocks that were on the move, shares of Albemarle (ALB) were +2.3% while Robinhood Markets (HOOD) was -2.8%.

data.title: Regional banks take charge in 2025, outperforming the broader financial sector and S&P 500
data.description:
The regional banking industry, a key pillar of the financial industry, delivered a notably strong performance in 2025, surpassing both the broader sector and the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500). Improved credit conditions, resilient consumer activity, and stabilizing funding costs helped support sector-wide gains throughout the year.

As 2025 draws to a close, regional banks have emerged as clear standouts, generating a year-to-date return of +19.2% as a group. That performance exceeds the financial sector’s +14.5% advance and outpaces the S&P 500’s +16.5% gain, underscoring renewed investor confidence in the segment. 

Against this backdrop, the following highlights the 10 top-performing regional bank stocks of 2025. To ensure comparability, the list is limited to companies with market capitalizations of $1 billion or more. 

No. 1: Customers Bancorp (CUBI), +58.4% YTD.

No. 2: Flagstar Bank (FLG), +41.1% YTD.

No. 3: Coastal Financial (CCB), +39.1% YTD. 

No. 4: Citizens Financial Group (CFG), +36.8% YTD. 

No. 5: Popular (BPOP), +34.3% YTD.

No. 6: The Bancorp (TBBK), +33.8% YTD. 

No. 7: Valley National Bancorp (VLY), +33.3% YTD. 

No. 8: Bank First Corporation (BFC), +32.2% YTD.

No. 9: Banc of California (BANC), +28.7% YTD. 

No. 10: Cadence Bank (CADE), +28.3% YTD.

Regional Bank ETFs: (KRE), (KBWR), (IAT), and (DPST). 

Bank ETFs: (KBWB), (KBE), and (FTXO). 

Financials ETFs: (XLF), (VFH), (IYF), (FNCL), (IYG), and (FXO).
